items.title: Locals fundraising to stop beachside town slipping into the ocean

children.content: Lancelin residents have been left trying to fundraise $150,000 for a sea wall in a bid to fight coastal erosion now seriously threatening the town, more than 100 kilometres north of Perth.

items.title: The 'amazing' turnaround in literacy at this primary school

children.content: An inspiring turnaround at an Aboriginal community school could provide a scalable solution for closing the gap on Indigenous literacy.

image.altText: A girl sits on the floor leaning over a piece of paper that has a grid and words on it. She is looking up and smiling.

items.title: WA schools say changes to bus services threaten their future

children.content: The principal of an independent school in regional WA says changes to student travel subsidies could ultimately lead to the institution's demise.

image.altText: An orange school bus drives on a gravel road in Western Australia.

items.title: 'Completely unimaginable': Man critically injured in robot vacuum fire

children.content: The family of a Perth man critically injured when a robot vacuum caught fire at his home say their lives have been "turned upside down" by the "horrific" incident.

image.altText: A critically ill man is asleep in hospital with breathing tubes

items.title: Widow not told of husband's death on FIFO swing until 14 hours later

children.content: A Perth widow says she has been kept in the dark about the circumstances leading to the death of her husband as he worked on an offshore gas plant.

image.altText: Laurence smiles while he lies on the bed with his dog

items.title: Labor in 'fight for our lives' as by-election opens door to One Nation assault

children.content: Paul Papalia's resignation for family reasons sparks a by-election in the WA minister and Labor stalwart's southern Perth seat at a time when disgruntled voters are increasingly turning to One Nation.

articles.title: By-election to follow minister's resignation amid One Nation surge

children.content: WA Labor stalwart and senior state cabinet minister Paul Papalia has announced he is quitting politics after nearly two decades, triggering a by-election in his southern Perth seat.

items.title: PM prepares to host leaders of three Pacific nations in Brisbane

children.content: The prime minister will host the leaders of PNG, Tonga and Samoa in Brisbane today for regional security talks after signing of the Ocean of Peace Alliance with Fiji. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese at Pacific Islands Forum

items.title: Discounts 'too little, too late' after slow start to WA's northern tourist season

children.content: The WA government says a discounted tours scheme will help revitalise the Kimberley's sluggish start to its annual tourism season but operators have questioned the voucher program.

image.altText: A girl stands holding a suitcase at the end of the groyne at Town Beach in Broome looking out to the ocean

Switzerland players celebrate after winning a penalty shootout

articles.title: 'Perversion of justice': Politicians launch bid to investigate FIFA boss

children.content: A group of European politicians launches a bid to begin an investigation into how and why a red card for an American football star was overturned at the World Cup.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino in blue suits peering over the golden FIFA Club World Cup trophy on the Oval Office desk

articles.title: 'We have suffered injustice': Egypt coach fumes at ref after Argentina loss

children.content: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan says his side "suffered an injustice" in defeat to Argentina, raging at the referee for a number of perceived errors.

image.altText: Hossam Hassan yells in anger on the touchline

children.content: Lionel Messi guides Argentina to an incredible comeback win against Egypt, coming back from 2-0 down to steal a 3-2 win at the death, while Switzerland knocks Colombia out in a penalty shootout. Follow live.

items.title: Messi leads incredible Argentina comeback before Swiss shootout delight
China missile test hits a bullseye in the Pacific — but it's one they didn't want

children.content: This week, perhaps for the first time in a long time, some sections of the Pacific are signalling a slight adjustment to their relationship with China.

image.altText: A man in a suit in a hospital environment with young girls nearby and a solomon islands flag

items.title: 'Teeth can heal': Oral health giant's ad claims called into question

children.content: Oral care giant Hismile has used "misleading" videos in social media advertisements showing a sales professional, and its own staff, spruiking products while dressed as health workers.

image.altText: A composite of two scenes from a Hismile ad

items.title: Five podcasts we're listening to this NAIDOC Week

children.content: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

items.title: Does Alcoa's $8b acquisition leave WA's jarrah forests in the firing line?

children.content: Alcoa's multi-billion-dollar deal to buy South32's alumina operations could unlock swathes of Western Australia's native jarrah forests for bauxite mining, environmentalists say.

items.title: Government report says AI not causing mass job lay-offs, yet

children.content: Artificial intelligence is not yet causing a broad disruption to Australia's labour market, according to a government report.

items.title: AFL legend Tony Modra 'overwhelmed by the kindness' after truck crash

children.content: Former Adelaide Crows and Fremantle player Tony Modra says he has left hospital after he was seriously injured in a truck crash last month.

image.altText: A man wearing a black shirt smiles.

items.title: Teammates of Melbourne footballer will sit next match out after death

children.content: Epping football club's reserves players have decided to skip this weekend's game following the death of their beloved teammate Nathan Fitzgerald on Monday night.

items.title: Housing inequity in Australia 'must focus beyond age'

children.content: The federal government must look at age and wealth together to meaningfully deal with the growing wealth divide between generations, according to The Australian Council of Social Services (ACOSS).

image.altText: an outside view of a row of houses

items.title: 'Disgusting' antisemitic graffiti sprayed on Anzac landmark

children.content: Swastikas and anti-Jewish abuse were discovered spray-painted on rocks and the historic Plantagenet Battery along Western Australia's south coast.

items.title: Australia urged to sign treaty banning nuclear weapons

children.content: There are renewed calls for Australia to sign a treaty ensuring nuclear weapons can't ever be facilitated, stored or manufactured in Australia.

items.title: Visa woes behind him, Bryce Cotton stars for Boomers on return to Perth

children.content: Six-time NBL MVP Bryce Cotton shines as he pulls on Australian colours for the first time in the Boomers' FIBA World Cup qualifiers.

image.altText: Bryce Cotton shoots against the Philippines in a FIBA World Cup qualifier, arms outstretched with the ball going for goal.

items.title: Swimmer who disappeared from Perth beach 13 years ago likely killed by shark

children.content: Martyn Tann was last seen at Mullaloo Beach in 2013, with a coroner telling an inquest into his disappearance that she believed he was dead, likely in a shark attack.
